km.Cornwall, England: ខូរនវ៉ល, ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស

zh.lifeSummary: Cicely de Haye於大約1287年出生在英國可瓦爾。她於大約1305年與Ralph Trethaek在英國可瓦爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Cicely de Haye于大约1287年出生在英国可瓦爾。她于大约1305年与Ralph Trethaek在英国可瓦爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Haye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Helmut, Willi.

North German, East Frisian, and Dutch: from a personal name, Frisian Hajo, ancient Germanic Haio; or from a short form of the female name Hadewig, composed of the elements hadu ‘battle, strife’ + wīg ‘war’.

variants.name: Deshayes
meanings: French: topographic name for someone who lived by an enclosure, from the plural of Old French haye ‘hedge’ (see Haye and Hayes ), with fused preposition and definite article des ‘from the’; or a habitational name denoting someone from Les Hayes, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare Deshaies .

variants.name: Hayenga
meanings: East Frisian: patronymic from the personal name Hajo (see Haye 1). It is formed with the suffix -enga, a variant of -inga (from the patronymic suffix -ing and genitive plural ending -a).

variants.name: Hey
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Hay  .
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized (or archaic) and Flemish, of Heij, a topographic name for someone who lived on a heath, Dutch hei, heide.
German: metonymic occupational name for a grower or mower of grass, from Middle High German höu ‘grass, hay’.

variants.name: Hayen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphonse.  German Heinz, Wilhelm.
Flemish, East Frisian, and North German: patronymic from   Haye   1.

nameMeanings.name: Cicely
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Cecily .  This was a common form of the name in the Middle Ages, and in the 20th century was well known as the name of the British actress and singer  Cicely Courtneidge  (  1893–1980 ).

variants.name: Cecily
meanings: Medieval vernacular English form of   Cecilia .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: Cicely de Haye was born about 1287, in Cornwall, England. She married Ralph Trethaek about 1305, in Cornwall, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Cicely de Haye est née environ 1287, à Cornouailles, Angleterre. Elle a épousé Ralph Trethaek environ 1305, à Cornouailles, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Cicely de Haye wurde um 1287 in Cornwall, England geboren. Sie hat um 1305 in Cornwall, England Ralph Trethaek geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Cicely de Haye è nata il circa 1287 nel Cornwall, England. Lei sposato Ralph Trethaek circa 1305, nel Cornwall, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.lifeSummary: Cicely de Hayeは約1287年にイギリスコルンワルで生まれた。約1305年にイギリスコルンワルでRalph Trethaekと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Cicely de Haye(은)는 대략 1287년 영국 코른월에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1305년 영국 코른월에서 Ralph Trethaek(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Cicely de Haye nasceu aproximadamente 1287, em Cornwall, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Ralph Trethaek aproximadamente 1305, em Cornwall, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Cicely de Haye родилась около 1287 г. в местности корнвалл, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Ralph Trethaek около 1305 г. в местности корнвалл, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Cicely de Haye nació aproximadamente 1287, en Cornwall, Inglaterra. Se casó con Ralph Trethaek aproximadamente 1305, en Cornwall, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
